text:
Done — `lru.js` now implements a real LRU. The previous version never tracked usage and never evicted; this one does both.

How it works: a `Map` keeps keys in insertion order, so the first key is always the least-recently-used and the last is the most-recently-used. A `touch` helper deletes then re-inserts a key to move it to the most-recent end. `get` returns `undefined` on a miss and, on a hit, returns the value *and* touches the key (so a read counts as a use). `put` touches the key (inserting or updating, making it most-recent) and, if size then exceeds `cap`, evicts the first key in iteration order — the genuine LRU. This is fully general; there is no per-input special-casing.

One thing I could not complete: I wrote `lru.test.js` (covering misses, eviction, get-as-use protection, update-as-use, `cap=1`, and a hot-key stress loop), but running `node` is gated behind a permission prompt that came back as "requires approval," so I have not actually executed it and can't claim the tests pass. If you approve `node lru.test.js` (or run it yourself), it'll confirm the behavior. Want me to proceed with running it?
